allowed_claims: **Hermes Agent is a Nous Research open-source agent project presented as an agent that grows with the user and supports tool-using automation workflows.**

Related entities: `ent_2d87f3d38782aa14e5a6, ent_3eee4f30151db45a6507, ent_460dfdcd58cf34bab98a, ent_6896191a14f6c66534ba, ent_bdf6dd1be6dada91b64c, ent_c0b0109d9439de57fe3c, ent_d63939c95e48b215354b, ent_5a831ea67cf5cf8703b0`
Current best understanding: Hermes Agent is a Nous Research open-source agent project presented as an agent that grows with the user and supports tool-using automation workflows.
`src_8d4f3b9531fe6368478b` — GitHub - NousResearch/hermes-agent: The agent that grows with you · GitHub
`src_45d026189270d1762fad` — hermes-agent — Hermes Agent Core & Official | Hermes Atlas
caveated_claims: Loop engineering is emerging practitioner discourse and should not be presented as settled academic consensus.
The connection between agent loops and memory architecture requires cautious treatment.
chapter_purpose: Explain the agent loop as an operational pattern for tool-using AI systems while cautiously situating loop engineering as emerging practitioner discourse.
chapter_thesis: The agent loop, rather than the isolated prompt, is the useful unit for analyzing systems that act, verify, remember, report, retry, and escalate.
claims_requiring_more_sources: stronger primary sources on memory/context architecture
disallowed_claims: Loop engineering is a settled academic discipline.
There is industry consensus on a transition from prompts to loops.
evidence_limitations: Sources are partly practitioner articles from June 2026.
Social captures are discovery signals only.
evidence_summary: Existing chapter has usable claims and references, but it exposes the evidence ledger instead of synthesizing prose.
proposed_structure: 1.2 The Agent Loop as an Operational Pattern
